methods.description: Process for studying surface polygenetic soils and buried paleosols in river valleys and intermountain basins to understand landscape stability and paleoenvironmental conditions.

steps.description: Both surface polygenetic soils and buried paleosols of soil-sedimentary sequences located in the bottoms of big river valleys and within intermountain basins of Altai Mountains were studied.
steps.searchable_summary: collect soil and paleosol samples from specified locations

steps.description: Pedolithostratigraphy and soil morphology were studied at the field stage.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze soil stratigraphy and morphology in the field

methods.description: Micromorphological analysis of pedogenic processes using thin sections and polarizing microscopy.

steps.description: Further micromorphological diagnostics of pedogenic processes was accomplished in thin sections, under a polarizing Nikon E200 Pol microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: perform micromorphological analysis using polarizing microscope

methods.description: Radiocarbon dating of samples from buried soils to determine their minimal age using scintillation method.

steps.description: The radiocarbon dating of the samples from buried soils was performed by the scintillation method in the Institute of Geology and Mineralogy of the Siberian Branch of the Russian Academy of Sciences.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct radiocarbon dating using scintillation method

steps.description: The residual activity of carbon was measured on a Quantulus-1220 device.
steps.searchable_summary: measure residual carbon activity with Quantulus-1220
